title: rubus × inaequatiformis
intro: Rubus × inaequatiformis, also known as the hybrid salmonberry or thimbleberry, is a cross between Rubus idaeus (the European raspberry) and Rubus laciniatus (the cut-leaved raspberry). This plant is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, and it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals. The health benefits of Rubus × inaequatiformis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its berries can be eaten fresh or used in jams and preserves.
